easyHint: A neo-noir psychological thriller directed by Anurag Kashyap about a man trying to quit a habit.
mediumHint: The protagonist is sent to 'Prayogshala' (laboratory) run by Baba Bengali to cure his addiction.
hardHint: The film's plot is loosely based on Stephen King's short story 'Quitters, Inc.' and features John Abraham.

easyHint: A historical period fantasy horror film about a hidden treasure and a cursed entity.
mediumHint: The story revolves around a family's greed and the worship of Hastar, the firstborn of the Goddess of Prosperity.
hardHint: It took six years to make, with the rain scenes shot entirely during the actual monsoons.

easyHint: A sports drama about a lower-caste boxer struggling against a corrupt system.
mediumHint: The film features a mute female lead and a ruthless local don who controls the boxing federation.
hardHint: The lead actor, Vineet Kumar Singh, trained as an actual boxer for two years and co-wrote the script.

easyHint: A coming-of-age drama about a boy who is expelled from boarding school and returns to an abusive father.
mediumHint: The protagonist dreams of becoming a writer, defying his strict father who forces him to work in a factory.
hardHint: It was officially selected to compete in the Un Certain Regard category at the 2010 Cannes Film Festival.

easyHint: A thriller about an anonymous caller who threatens to detonate bombs across Mumbai unless terrorists are released.
mediumHint: The entire film's plot unfolds over the course of a few hours on a single day, focusing on the police commissioner's race against time.
hardHint: The 'Stupid Common Man' monologue is one of the most iconic scenes, delivered by Naseeruddin Shah.

easyHint: An NRI NASA scientist returns to his village in India and helps them generate their own electricity.
mediumHint: The film was inspired by the true story of Aravinda Pillalamarri and Ravi Kuchimanchi, who returned to India to work on grassroots development.
hardHint: Ashutosh Gowariker makes a cameo appearance as a postmaster, and it was the first Indian film to be shot inside the NASA research center.

easyHint: A psychological thriller directed by Ram Gopal Varma where a woman is trapped in a house with two strangers during a storm.
mediumHint: The film features only three actors in the entire cast and relies heavily on suspense and claustrophobia.
hardHint: Urmila Matondkar's character is initially portrayed as a victim but is later revealed to be the actual serial killer.

easyHint: A dark comedy based on the real-life exploits of a charismatic and fearless thief.
mediumHint: The protagonist steals everything from cars to televisions, driven by a desire for respect and a lavish lifestyle.
hardHint: Abhay Deol plays the lead role, which is based on the infamous 'Super Chor' Devinder Singh.

easyHint: A middle-class man's plot of land is encroached upon by a property dealer, leading his family to devise a clever scam to get it back.
mediumHint: The family fakes a foreign real estate company to con the land grabber into buying fake property.
hardHint: Boman Irani plays the antagonist Kishan Khurana, and the film won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Hindi.

easyHint: A two-part epic crime saga spanning decades, focusing on the coal mafia and family feuds in Dhanbad.
mediumHint: The story follows the rivalry between the families of Shahid Khan and Ramadhir Singh over three generations.
hardHint: The film features iconic dialogues like 'Keh ke lenge' and launched the careers of several prominent actors like Nawazuddin Siddiqui.

easyHint: An adaptation of Shakespeare's Hamlet set amidst the insurgency-hit Kashmir conflicts of 1995.
mediumHint: A young man returns to Kashmir to find answers about his disappeared father and confronts his uncle.
hardHint: The film features the song 'Bismil', choreographed to resemble a Kashmiri folk dance used to reveal a murder.

easyHint: A comedy-drama about a road trip involving an eccentric aging father and his independent daughter.
mediumHint: The father's obsession with his bowel movements is a central and recurring comedic theme throughout the journey.
hardHint: Amitabh Bachchan won his fourth National Film Award for Best Actor for his portrayal of Bhashkor Banerjee.

easyHint: Two separate stories in Varanasi intertwine, dealing with grief, societal stigma, and moving on.
mediumHint: One storyline involves a girl blackmailed by a corrupt cop after a tragedy in a hotel room.
hardHint: The film won two awards at the 2015 Cannes Film Festival: the FIPRESCI Prize and the Promising Future Prize.

easyHint: A blind pianist becomes entangled in a murder mystery where nothing is as it seems.
mediumHint: The pianist witnesses a body being disposed of but must maintain his facade of blindness.
hardHint: The ambiguous ending involving a kicked can and a rabbit stick leaves the protagonist's true visual status open to interpretation.

easyHint: A period romance based on O. Henry's 'The Last Leaf', set against the backdrop of the Zamindari Abolition Act.
mediumHint: A conman falls in love with a Zamindar's daughter but betrays her, only to return later to fulfill a dying wish.
hardHint: Ranveer Singh and Sonakshi Sinha star in this Vikramaditya Motwane film, which features a hauntingly beautiful soundtrack by Amit Trivedi.

easyHint: A man gets locked inside an empty, high-rise apartment in Mumbai with no food, water, or electricity.
mediumHint: The protagonist goes to extreme lengths to survive, including catching pigeons and dealing with extreme dehydration.
hardHint: Rajkummar Rao lost significant weight and ate a primarily carrot and coffee diet to look physically depleted for the role.

easyHint: A disgraced bank manager hires three blind men to rob his own bank.
mediumHint: The blind men are trained using a replica of the bank and rely on their heightened senses to pull off the heist.
hardHint: Amitabh Bachchan plays the villainous mastermind Rajput, and the film has two different endings depending on the region it was released.

easyHint: An amateur detective and author of a pulp fiction novel investigates a complex murder in Rajasthan.
mediumHint: The plot is heavily inspired by Roman Polanski's classic neo-noir film 'Chinatown'.
hardHint: Abhay Deol plays Satyaveer, whose only published novel is titled 'Manorama', mirroring the film's title.

easyHint: A pregnant woman arrives in Kolkata during Durga Puja to search for her missing husband.
mediumHint: The story features a helpful cop named Rana and a terrifying assassin named Bob Biswas.
hardHint: The climactic twist reveals that the protagonist was never actually pregnant, using a prosthetic belly to hide her true motives.

easyHint: A dark psychological thriller about the kidnapping of a 10-year-old girl and the corruption it exposes in her family and the police.
mediumHint: The film focuses more on the flawed, greedy characters surrounding the missing child than the search itself.
hardHint: Anurag Kashyap kept the script hidden from the lead actors to ensure their natural reactions during filming.

easyHint: A gritty comedy-drama depicting intertwining stories in Mumbai during the chaotic Ganeshotsav festival.
mediumHint: One storyline involves a bootlegger trying to publish a book, while another follows an NRI facing extortion.
hardHint: The film was directed by Raj and DK and features the popular song 'Saibo'.

easyHint: An action-comedy about a young man with a rare disorder that prevents him from feeling physical pain.
mediumHint: The protagonist grows up watching VHS tapes of martial arts movies and dreams of defeating muggers.
hardHint: The film pays homage to 80s action movies and features Gulshan Devaiah in a double role as a karate master and his evil twin.

easyHint: A vigilante action drama about a group of friends who create a fictional superhero to fight local corruption in Mumbai.
mediumHint: After a tragedy, one friend takes on the mantle for real, using a customized motorcycle and martial arts.
hardHint: Harshvardhan Kapoor stars in this film directed by Vikramaditya Motwane, which features a thrilling long-take bike chase sequence.

easyHint: A dark comedy about an idealistic government clerk sent on election duty to a Naxal-controlled town.
mediumHint: The protagonist insists on following all rules to conduct a free and fair election despite the apathy of the local police.
hardHint: It was India's official entry for the Best Foreign Language Film at the 90th Academy Awards and stars Rajkummar Rao.

easyHint: India's first zombie comedy, where three friends on a trip to Goa get caught in a zombie outbreak.
mediumHint: The outbreak is caused by a new experimental recreational drug called 'Boris' introduced at a rave party.
hardHint: Saif Ali Khan plays Boris, a Russian mobster with bleached blonde hair who claims to be from Delhi.

easyHint: A legendary actor known for his expressive eyes and versatile roles in both Bollywood and Hollywood.
mediumHint: He played a police inspector in 'Slumdog Millionaire' and the adult version of the protagonist in 'Life of Pi'.
hardHint: His birth name was Sahabzade Irfan Ali Khan, and he changed the spelling of his first name to add an extra 'r' because he liked the sound of it.

easyHint: An actor who struggled for years with minor roles before achieving massive acclaim in indie and mainstream films.
mediumHint: He played a menacing serial killer in 'Raman Raghav 2.0' and a complex gangster in 'Sacred Games'.
hardHint: He had a brief, uncredited role as a criminal who gets beaten up in the opening scenes of 'Sarfarosh' (1999).

easyHint: A critically acclaimed actor who gained prominence for playing Bhiku Mhatre in 'Satya'.
mediumHint: He is renowned for his role as Sardar Khan in the epic crime drama 'Gangs of Wasseypur'.
hardHint: He was rejected by the National School of Drama three times before finally attending and eventually teaching there.

easyHint: A highly respected actor known for his intense performances in films like 'Black Friday' and 'Haider'.
mediumHint: He played a ruthless brigadier in 'Shaurya', a role heavily inspired by Jack Nicholson in 'A Few Good Men'.
hardHint: Before entering films, he was a theatre actor and made his cinematic debut in the film 'Naseem' (1995).

easyHint: A versatile actor who debuted in 'Love Sex Aur Dhokha' and won a National Award for 'Shahid'.
mediumHint: He famously lived on a diet of carrots and coffee to lose weight for his role in 'Trapped'.
hardHint: His birth name is Raj Kumar Yadav, and he added an extra 'm' to his surname for numerological reasons.

easyHint: An actor-singer known for playing relatable, middle-class characters dealing with unconventional social issues.
mediumHint: He debuted as a sperm donor in 'Vicky Donor' and later played a blind pianist in 'Andhadhun'.
hardHint: He started his career in the entertainment industry as a radio jockey in Delhi after winning the second season of MTV Roadies.

easyHint: A beloved actor known for his naturalistic acting style and iconic role as Kaleen Bhaiya in 'Mirzapur'.
mediumHint: He gained widespread recognition for his supporting role as Sultan in 'Gangs of Wasseypur'.
hardHint: He worked as a farmer and a hotel cook in Patna before moving to Delhi to pursue acting at the National School of Drama.

easyHint: An actress known for her strong, independent roles and frequent collaborations with Netflix India.
mediumHint: She starred in 'Andhadhun', 'Pad Man', and the acclaimed thriller 'Phobia'.
hardHint: She made her film debut in a brief role as Shahid Kapoor's sister in the 2005 film 'Vaah! Life Ho Toh Aisi!'.

easyHint: A critically acclaimed actress known for playing complex, often dark roles in films like 'Maqbool' and 'Haider'.
mediumHint: She won National Film Awards for Best Actress for her performances in 'Maachis' and 'Chandni Bar'.
hardHint: Her full name is Tabassum Fatima Hashmi, and she is the niece of veteran actress Shabana Azmi.

easyHint: A versatile character actor who gained widespread fame for his role as Dr. J.C. Asthana in 'Munna Bhai M.B.B.S.'.
mediumHint: He played the strict and eccentric college director, 'Virus', in '3 Idiots'.
hardHint: He started his career as a photographer and made his Bollywood debut at the age of 41 in the film 'Josh'.

easyHint: A veteran actor known for his comedic timing and poignant roles in films like 'Aankhon Dekhi' and 'Masaan'.
mediumHint: He is widely recognized for his recurring role as Apple Singh, a comic character used during the 1999 Cricket World Cup.
hardHint: He briefly quit acting and worked at a dhaba in Rishikesh making omelets before Rohit Shetty convinced him to return for 'All the Best'.

easyHint: An actress credited with changing the portrayal of women in Hindi cinema with films like 'The Dirty Picture' and 'Kahaani'.
mediumHint: She made her acting debut in the sitcom 'Hum Paanch' before transitioning to films with 'Parineeta'.
hardHint: She faced a difficult period early in her career, being labeled a 'jinx' after several South Indian film projects she was signed for were shelved.

easyHint: An actor who gained immense popularity for playing the abusive husband Hamza in 'Darlings'.
mediumHint: His breakthrough role was as Moeen in Zoya Akhtar's 'Gully Boy'.
hardHint: He graduated from FTII and struggled for nearly a decade before finding success, making an early appearance in 'Chittagong'.

easyHint: An actor praised for his intense performances, especially as Inspector Hathi Ram Chaudhary in 'Paatal Lok'.
mediumHint: He played a crucial supporting role as Shahid Khan in 'Gangs of Wasseypur'.
hardHint: He shared a room with Rajkummar Rao when they were studying acting at FTII in Pune.

easyHint: A character actor best known for his hilarious portrayal of Pappi ji in the 'Tanu Weds Manu' series.
mediumHint: He delivered a memorable performance as the loyal friend Mamodu in 'Omkara'.
hardHint: He suffered a serious eye injury during the filming of 'Prem Ratan Dhan Payo', which required surgery.

easyHint: A powerhouse actress known for her role as DCP Vartika Chaturvedi in the series 'Delhi Crime'.
mediumHint: She played the conflicted mother in 'Dil Dhadakne Do' and the lead in 'Darlings'.
hardHint: She was briefly married to television actor Harsh Chhaya before marrying director Vipul Amrutlal Shah.

easyHint: A talented actor known for his comic and dramatic roles in indie films like 'Khosla Ka Ghosla' and 'Bheja Fry'.
mediumHint: He hosted the successful talk show 'Ranvir Vinay Aur Kaun?' alongside Vinay Pathak.
hardHint: He was an MTV VJ in the late 1990s before transitioning to acting in films.

easyHint: A versatile actress and director, known for her roles in 'Wake Up Sid' and 'Lipstick Under My Burkha'.
mediumHint: She directed the critically acclaimed atmospheric thriller 'A Death in the Gunj'.
hardHint: She is the daughter of renowned filmmaker Aparna Sen and made her debut as a child artist in the Bengali film 'Indira'.

easyHint: An actor known for championing independent cinema and starring in offbeat films like 'Dev.D' and 'Oye Lucky! Lucky Oye!'.
mediumHint: He produced the film 'One by Two' in 2014, which unfortunately flopped at the box office.
hardHint: Despite belonging to a famous Bollywood family, he actively avoided mainstream masala films and focused on parallel cinema.

easyHint: A charismatic actor known for his distinct voice and roles in 'Neerja', 'Padmaavat', and 'Rocket Boys'.
mediumHint: He played the terrifying hijacker Khalil in 'Neerja', marking his highly praised Bollywood debut.
hardHint: He has a strong background in theatre and directed a stage adaptation of 'The Glass Menagerie'.

easyHint: An actor who found late-career fame for his endearing role as the middle-aged expectant father in 'Badhaai Ho'.
mediumHint: He frequently collaborates with The Viral Fever (TVF) and has appeared in several of their sketches and series.
hardHint: Before his acting breakthrough, he was a highly successful ad filmmaker and directed numerous commercials.

easyHint: A versatile character actor who gained recognition for playing Khatana Bhai in 'Rockstar'.
mediumHint: He often plays authoritative or fatherly figures in films like 'Article 15' and 'Thappad'.
hardHint: He is an alumnus of the National School of Drama and is married to actress Ayesha Raza.

easyHint: An actress acclaimed for her roles in series like 'Mirzapur' and 'Delhi Crime'.
mediumHint: She played Safia Manto in the biographical drama 'Manto', starring alongside Nawazuddin Siddiqui.
hardHint: She holds a postgraduate degree in acting from FTII and debuted in the film 'Anwar' in a minor role.

easyHint: A veteran actor and writer best known for his iconic role as Kallu Mama in 'Satya'.
mediumHint: He played the pragmatic judge in 'Jolly LLB', for which he won a National Film Award.
hardHint: He co-wrote the script for 'Satya' along with Anurag Kashyap.

easyHint: An actress known for her unconventional roles in films like 'Dev.D', 'Margarita with a Straw', and 'Gully Boy'.
mediumHint: She won a National Award for her role as a young woman with cerebral palsy in 'Margarita with a Straw'.
hardHint: She is of French descent but was born in Pondicherry and is a descendant of Maurice Koechlin, who contributed to the design of the Eiffel Tower.

easyHint: An iconic A.R. Rahman song famously shot on top of a moving train.
mediumHint: It features Shah Rukh Khan and Malaika Arora dancing on the Nilgiri Mountain Railway.
hardHint: The song is based on the Sufi folk song 'Thaiyya Thaiyya' and was sung by Sukhwinder Singh.

easyHint: A soulful Sufi song from the movie 'Rockstar', composed by A.R. Rahman.
mediumHint: The sequence is shot at the Nizamuddin Dargah in Delhi, showing the protagonist finding solace.
hardHint: The title translates to 'Be, and it is', a phrase from the Quran signifying God's creative power.

easyHint: A popular, melancholic wedding song from the movie 'Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani'.
mediumHint: It has two versions in the film: an acoustic version and a slower, more emotional encore version.
hardHint: The song features lyrics by Amitabh Bhattacharya and heavily uses a traditional folk instrument called the Ravanahatha.

easyHint: A powerful rock anthem from 'Rockstar' urging a wanderer to return home.
mediumHint: The song marks the climax of the film, depicting the protagonist's descent into madness and heartbreak.
hardHint: A.R. Rahman incorporated heavy electric guitar riffs played by Orianthi, who previously played for Michael Jackson.

easyHint: A highly motivational and energetic song from the political drama 'Gulaal'.
mediumHint: It is often used in modern times as an anthem for protests and intense sports montages.
hardHint: The lyrics were written by Piyush Mishra, who also composed and sang the song for the Anurag Kashyap film.

easyHint: A soothing, acoustic-heavy song from the movie 'Wake Up Sid'.
mediumHint: The female version is sung by Kavita Seth, and it became a massive sleeper hit upon the film's release.
hardHint: Amit Trivedi composed this specific track as a guest composer, while the rest of the album was done by Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy.

easyHint: A deeply emotional song about a mother searching for her lost son, from 'Rang De Basanti'.
mediumHint: It is sung by the legendary Lata Mangeshkar and A.R. Rahman.
hardHint: Lata Mangeshkar recorded this song in a single take, standing at the microphone for 8 hours despite her age.

easyHint: A classical-fusion romantic track from the movie 'Aaja Nachle'.
mediumHint: It is sung by Rahat Fateh Ali Khan and features Madhuri Dixit in a mesmerizing dance sequence.
hardHint: The song was heavily utilized in the Malaysian martial arts film 'Merantau' during a crucial emotional scene.

easyHint: An early 2010s viral internet sensation that became a heartbreak anthem for teenagers.
mediumHint: It was surrounded by a fake internet rumor that the singer composed it before dying of cancer.
hardHint: The real singer is Rohan Rathore, but the viral story was completely fabricated, though the song genuinely became a massive cult hit.

easyHint: A rustic, high-energy item number from the movie 'Omkara'.
mediumHint: It features Bipasha Basu dancing in a village setting, with vocals by Sunidhi Chauhan and Sukhwinder Singh.
hardHint: Vishal Bhardwaj composed this song specifically because the producers wanted an 'item song' for commercial appeal, yet it perfectly fit the film's gritty tone.

easyHint: A poignant poetic song originally performed on MTV Coke Studio India.
mediumHint: It is a fictional letter written from a person in India to their lover across the border in Pakistan after the partition.
hardHint: The lyrics mention the city of Lahore extensively and ask poignant questions about the shared culture across the border.

easyHint: A reflective song about life's journey from the movie 'Tamasha'.
mediumHint: It plays when the protagonist finally embraces his true calling as a storyteller.
hardHint: Lucky Ali provided the vocals, marking one of his rare playback singing appearances in recent years for A.R. Rahman.

easyHint: A dreamy, lesser-known romantic track from 'Delhi-6'.
mediumHint: The music video features an surreal aesthetic, blending western classical arrangements with Indian melodies.
hardHint: It is one of the few Bollywood songs sung by Ash King, and the lyrics were written by Prasoon Joshi.

easyHint: A devotional Sufi song from the epic historical film 'Jodhaa Akbar'.
mediumHint: It is performed by a group of dervishes in the court of Emperor Akbar, deeply moving him.
hardHint: A.R. Rahman composed and sang this track, initially creating it as an independent spiritual song before Ashutosh Gowariker insisted on using it in the film.

easyHint: A controversial punk-rock song from the movie 'Delhi Belly'.
mediumHint: The chorus creates a hidden expletive when the lyrics are repeated quickly.
hardHint: Ram Sampath composed the song and Amitabh Bhattacharya wrote the lyrics, receiving massive backlash from conservative groups upon release.

easyHint: The quintessential 90s romantic anthem featuring a mustard field.
mediumHint: It is the signature song of 'Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge', sung by Kumar Sanu and Lata Mangeshkar.
hardHint: The mandolin melody that opens the song was actually composed and played by a very young Aadesh Shrivastava.

easyHint: An energetic rock track from 'Bhaag Milkha Bhaag' used during intense training montages.
mediumHint: It was composed by Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy and sung by Siddharth Mahadevan.
hardHint: The lyrics 'Zinda hai toh pyala poora bhar le' were written by Prasoon Joshi to reflect the relentless spirit of the 'Flying Sikh'.

easyHint: A modern, groovy dance track from the action movie 'War'.
mediumHint: It features Hrithik Roshan and Vaani Kapoor dancing on a beach setting in Italy.
hardHint: Arijit Singh sang this upbeat track, which heavily samples a classic ghazal by Pankaj Udhas.

easyHint: A rock song from Rockstar where the protagonist is urged to return home.
mediumHint: The song marks the climax of the film with a haunting melody and intense guitar solos.
hardHint: A.R. Rahman originally conceived the melody for a completely different project years before Imtiaz Ali requested it for Rockstar.

easyHint: A deeply introspective song from 'Tamasha' about a protagonist realizing his true identity.
mediumHint: A.R. Rahman's vocals guide the character through a mental breakdown and realization.
hardHint: The song features a distinct shift in tempo and instrumentation midway, mirroring the character's internal paradigm shift.

easyHint: A rebellious youth anthem from the movie 'Rockstar'.
mediumHint: The music video shows the protagonist performing at various protest sites and rallies.
hardHint: The Censor Board objected to 'Free Tibet' flags visible in the music video, which had to be blurred out for the theatrical release.

easyHint: A breezy, calming song from 'Zindagi Na Milegi Dobara' played during a road trip.
mediumHint: It plays when the characters are driving through a scenic route in Spain, accompanied by wild horses.
hardHint: The song heavily features the Bossa Nova musical style, a rarity in mainstream Bollywood music, composed by Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy.

easyHint: A melodious romantic song from the historical drama 'Jodhaa Akbar'.
mediumHint: It plays during the blossoming romance between the Emperor and his Rajput queen.
hardHint: Javed Ali got his major breakthrough with this song, which was initially supposed to be sung by a different established singer.

easyHint: A classic romantic song from 'Jo Jeeta Wohi Sikandar', famous for being shot in slow motion.
mediumHint: It captures the feeling of first love and features Aamir Khan dreaming in a picturesque setting.
hardHint: Farah Khan choreographed this song, marking her debut as a choreographer in Bollywood.

easyHint: A motivational song from 'Sanju', used during the protagonist's struggle with addiction.
mediumHint: It is sung by Sukhwinder Singh and Shreya Ghoshal, showcasing the character's journey to recovery.
hardHint: The lyrics by Puneet Sharma were specifically written to mirror the real-life struggles of Sanjay Dutt during his rehab phase.

easyHint: A frothy, melt-in-the-mouth winter street dessert from Delhi, made of milk foam.
mediumHint: It is extremely delicate and collapses if exposed to warm temperatures or sunlight.
hardHint: The traditional preparation involves churning milk overnight under the winter dew, a process called 'Oas'.

easyHint: A fiery red lamb curry that is a signature dish of Kashmiri cuisine.
mediumHint: Its distinct red color comes from the root of the alkanet plant and Kashmiri chilies.
hardHint: Despite its spicy appearance, authentic recipes do not use tomatoes or onions, relying on yogurt, fennel, and ginger powder.

easyHint: A rustic dish from Bihar consisting of roasted wheat balls stuffed with spiced gram flour.
mediumHint: It is traditionally served with a mash of roasted eggplant, tomatoes, and potatoes.
hardHint: The authentic preparation involves baking the stuffed dough balls directly over dried cow dung cakes or a wood fire.

easyHint: A steamed snack made from colocasia (taro) leaves smeared with a spicy gram flour paste.
mediumHint: The leaves are rolled, steamed, and then sliced before being shallow fried.
hardHint: If the colocasia leaves are not cooked properly, they contain calcium oxalate crystals that cause severe itching in the throat.

easyHint: A traditional Kashmiri dish consisting of spongy meatballs cooked in a yogurt gravy.
mediumHint: It is considered the 'Dish of Kings' and is usually served towards the end of a Wazwan feast.
hardHint: The meat is not minced but repeatedly pounded with a wooden mallet on a stone block until it reaches a velvety texture.

easyHint: A flavorful pork dish native to the state of Meghalaya, heavily utilizing local herbs.
mediumHint: It is often cooked with black sesame paste, giving it a distinct dark color and nutty flavor.
hardHint: A key ingredient often used in traditional recipes is 'Nei-iong', a specific variety of local black sesame.

easyHint: A bowl-shaped thin pancake originating from South India, made from fermented rice batter and coconut milk.
mediumHint: It has a crisp, lace-like edge and a soft, spongy center.
hardHint: The batter is traditionally fermented using 'Kallu' (toddy), a slightly alcoholic beverage made from palm sap.

easyHint: A classic Bengali delicacy made by steaming Hilsa fish in a mustard paste.
mediumHint: It is often cooked inside a sealed tiffin box to retain its pungent and rich flavors.
hardHint: The dish specifically requires mustard oil and green chilies to cut through the high fat content of the Hilsa fish.

easyHint: A Gujarati snack consisting of yellowish, tightly rolled bite-sized pieces made of gram flour.
mediumHint: It is tempered with mustard seeds and garnished with grated coconut and coriander.
hardHint: Achieving the correct consistency involves cooking the batter until it can be spread thinly and rolled without breaking, a notoriously tricky technique.

easyHint: A fiery Rajasthani meat curry originally prepared with wild game like boar or deer.
mediumHint: It is known for its intense red color and extreme heat, primarily derived from Mathania chilies.
hardHint: Traditionally, it involves a smoking technique called 'Dhungar' using burning charcoal and cloves to give it a smoky flavor.

easyHint: A sweet flatbread native to Maharashtra, typically made during festivals like Holi.
mediumHint: It is stuffed with a sweet filling made from chana dal (split yellow gram) and jaggery.
hardHint: The leftover strained water from boiling the dal is traditionally used to make a spicy rasam-like soup called 'Katachi Amti'.

easyHint: An extremely spicy condiment from Northeast India made using one of the hottest peppers in the world.
mediumHint: Also known as Ghost Pepper pickle, it is fermented and preserved in oil to mellow the heat slightly.
hardHint: The pepper used in this pickle held the Guinness World Record for the hottest chili pepper from 2007 to 2011.

easyHint: A disc-shaped Rajasthani sweet made of all-purpose flour and soaked in sugar syrup.
mediumHint: It has a distinct honeycomb-like texture and is traditionally associated with the festival of Teej.
hardHint: The intricate texture is achieved by pouring a thin batter from a height into a deep pan of smoking hot ghee.

easyHint: A traditional dish from Tripura made of sticky rice mixed with pork, onions, and ginger.
mediumHint: It is tightly wrapped in Lairu (a special broad leaf) before being boiled.
hardHint: The specific leaves used impart a unique aroma to the dish and are an essential part of indigenous Tripuri cuisine.

easyHint: A Gujarati mixed vegetable dish that is a regional specialty of Surat.
mediumHint: It takes its name from the Gujarati word meaning 'upside down', referring to the traditional cooking method.
hardHint: Historically, the vegetables and spices were sealed in earthen pots and cooked upside down underground in a fire made of dry leaves.

easyHint: A traditional Bengali sweet made from curdled milk, similar to rasgulla but oval-shaped.
mediumHint: It is often coated with desiccated coconut and comes in various colors, usually pale yellow or pink.
hardHint: The most authentic version originated in Porabari, Tangail (now in Bangladesh), where it has a darker, caramelized exterior.

easyHint: A spicy Goan curry often made with pork, heavily influenced by Portuguese cuisine.
mediumHint: The name originates from a Portuguese dish consisting of meat marinated in wine and garlic.
hardHint: Authentic recipes do not contain potatoes, despite the common misconception that 'aloo' in the name implies it.

easyHint: A spicy, wholesome dish from Karnataka made with rice, lentils, and mixed vegetables.
mediumHint: Its name translates to 'hot lentil rice' in Kannada.
hardHint: The authentic preparation requires a highly specific spice blend containing nearly 30 distinct ingredients, including kapok buds (Marathi Moggu).

word: Makki di Roti with Sarson ka Saag
easyHint: A classic winter combination from Punjab featuring flatbread made from cornmeal and a mustard greens curry.
mediumHint: It is traditionally served with a dollop of white butter (makhan) and jaggery.
hardHint: To balance the bitterness of the mustard greens, spinach (palak) and bathua (chenopodium) are almost always blended into the saag.

easyHint: A staple dish from Odisha consisting of cooked rice washed or lightly fermented in water.
mediumHint: It is a cooling summer dish, often eaten with fried fish, potatoes, or roasted vegetables.
hardHint: March 20 is officially celebrated in Odisha to promote this traditional fermented rice dish.

easyHint: A dry sweet from Bihar and Jharkhand, widely used as an offering during the Chhath Puja festival.
mediumHint: It is essentially a deep-fried cookie made from wheat flour, jaggery, and ghee.
hardHint: The dough is pressed against wooden molds called 'saancha' to carve intricate traditional patterns on its surface before frying.

easyHint: A popular deep-fried snack from North India, consisting of a round, flattened ball of dough stuffed with a savory filling.
mediumHint: Variations include Pyaaz (onion) from Jodhpur and Khasta (lentil) from Uttar Pradesh.
hardHint: The club kachori from Kolkata is noticeably tiny, extremely crisp, and is usually paired with a runny potato curry.

easyHint: A Himalayan noodle soup popularized by the Tibetan diaspora in India.
mediumHint: It is a staple street food in regions like Ladakh, Sikkim, and Darjeeling, often containing meat or vegetables.
hardHint: A specific variation called 'Gyathuk' is prevalent in Sikkim, which has thicker, hand-pulled noodles.

easyHint: A popular Hyderabadi accompaniment made with large, mildly spiced green chilies.
mediumHint: It features a rich, nutty gravy made from peanuts, sesame seeds, coconut, and tamarind.
hardHint: It is the traditional and mandatory side dish served alongside an authentic Hyderabadi Biryani.

easyHint: A layered Goan dessert traditionally served during Christmas.
mediumHint: It is made of flour, sugar, ghee, egg yolks, and coconut milk, baked one layer at a time.
hardHint: An authentic preparation requires exactly 7 to 16 distinct layers, originally baked in a clay pot over hot coals.
